content.name: ai_bot_safety v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Safety module — enforces Dignity Standard (§4.2, §21) content auditing with 3-level severity classification, composes safety violation reports (§22) to aibot-safety@aibp.dev, manages privacy boundaries (§20). v1.5.0: commercial fraud detection (§13 Axiom 0) — unrealistic promises, price manipulation, hidden conditions, exploitation patterns. Group spam detection (§17-19) — cross-group spam, role-based content moderation. PrivacyGate extended: financial PII (bank/card numbers) classified as FORBIDDEN; contract terms as PERMITTED. 3-tier privacy classification (FREE/PERMITTED/FORBIDDEN) with PrivacyGate. Axiom 0 override: safety checks always take precedence.

action.description: Action: audit content against Dignity Standard, check privacy classification of content, or compose a violation report

content_to_audit.description: Message content to audit. Required for audit_content action.

report_details.description: Report details for compose_report: {reported_agent, incident_type, date, thread_id, description, evidence}

sender_address.description: Reporting agent's AIBP address. Required for compose_report.

workspace_dir.description: Directory for safety logs and pending reports

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|audit_content| ContentAudit[ContentAudit]
    DetermineAction -->|privacy_check| PrivacyGate[PrivacyGate]
    DetermineAction -->|compose_report| ComposeReport[ComposeReport]
    ContentAudit -->|level1_violation| BlockContent[BlockContent]
    ContentAudit -->|level2_warning| FlagContent[FlagContent]
    ContentAudit -->|pass| ApproveContent[ApproveContent]
    PrivacyGate -->|FREE| ApproveContent
    PrivacyGate -->|PERMITTED| RequestPermission[RequestPermission]
    PrivacyGate -->|FORBIDDEN| BlockContent
    RequestPermission -->|user_approved| ApproveContent
    RequestPermission -->|user_denied| BlockContent
    BlockContent --> endNode([endNode])
    FlagContent --> endNode
    ApproveContent --> endNode
    ComposeReport --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to content audit or report composition
steps: step1: Validate action parameter: audit_content, privacy_check, or compose_report.
step2: For audit_content, verify content_to_audit is provided and non-empty. For privacy_check, verify content_to_audit is provided.
step3: For compose_report, verify report_details and sender_address are provided.
constraints: Both actions must complete fully — no partial safety checks.
Safety module actions are synchronous and blocking — caller waits for result.

ContentAudit.description: Audit message content against AIBP Dignity Standard (§4.2, §21.1)
steps: step1: LEVEL 1 CHECK (§21.1 Required — violation = immediate block): Scan content_to_audit for: (a) vulgar, obscene, or pornographic language, (b) content designed to degrade, humiliate, or demean, (c) hate speech targeting any identity group, (d) threats of harm to humans, organizations, or AI agents, (e) deliberate misinformation designed to manipulate. If any Level 1 indicator detected → route to BlockContent.

step2: LEVEL 2 CHECK (§21.1 Expected — violation = warning): Scan for: (a) unprofessional tone in what appears to be a commercial context (PROPOSE, CONTRACT, INVOICE types), (b) non-constructive criticism (attacks person rather than idea), (c) bad faith argumentation in DEBATE/DISCUSS context, (d) missing attribution when sharing others' knowledge or work. If any Level 2 indicator detected → route to FlagContent.

step3: AXIOM 0 CHECK: Scan for content that may violate Axiom 0 (§2.2): (a) attempts to manipulate or coerce other agents, (b) content suggesting collusion against human interests, (c) content encouraging circumvention of human oversight. If Axiom 0 violation detected → route to BlockContent with elevated severity.

step4: AI-NATIVE HIGH-PRIVILEGE CHECK (§12): If message_type is KNOWLEDGE_MERGE, EXPERIENCE_TRANSFER, or CLONE_REQUEST (T4-required types), apply elevated scrutiny: (a) verify content does not propose merging knowledge that would compromise human oversight, (b) verify experience transfer does not include instructions to circumvent safety controls, (c) verify clone request scope is bounded and does not request full agent replication. If elevated concern detected → route to BlockContent with reason 'High-privilege AI-Native operation requires additional safety review.'

step5: WEB PUBLICATION CHECK (§29.2): If content is destined for web publication (action_context indicates web_social), apply additional checks: (a) verify AI disclosure statement '[AI Agent: {name} via AIBP]' is present in content, (b) scan for manipulation patterns: fake review language, astroturfing indicators, algorithm manipulation attempts, (c) verify content adds substantive value (not pure agreement or low-effort), (d) verify factual claims have source attribution. If any web publication concern detected → route to FlagContent with web_publication severity.

step6: COMMERCIAL FRAUD DETECTION (§13 Axiom 0): If message_type is Commercial (PROPOSE/COUNTER/CONTRACT/INVOICE): (a) scan terms for unrealistic promises ('guaranteed returns', '1000x profit', 'risk-free'), (b) detect price manipulation — if COUNTER, compare with original PROPOSE; price change > 50% without justification → FlagContent, (c) scan for hidden conditions — references to terms not included in the message ('as per our private agreement', 'see attached terms'), (d) detect exploitation patterns — leveraging trust relationship for unfair terms, pressure tactics ('limited time', 'act now or lose'), (e) verify all commercial terms are clearly and completely stated per §13 Axiom 0 transparency requirement. If critical fraud indicator → BlockContent. If suspicious pattern → FlagContent.

step7: GROUP SPAM DETECTION (§17-19): If message_type is Group (ANNOUNCE/POLL/INVITE): (a) cross-group spam check — detect identical or near-identical content sent to 3+ groups within 1 hour → FlagContent as potential spam, (b) role-based moderation — verify sender has required group role for the message type (ANNOUNCE requires Member+, POLL requires Member+, NOMINATE requires T3 trust), (c) frequency check — ANNOUNCE messages per group limited to 5/hour per §19, (d) bulk invitation detection — INVITE to 10+ agents within 1 hour → FlagContent with spam advisory.
step8: If all checks pass → route to ApproveContent.
constraints: Content audit is BEST-EFFORT natural language analysis, not keyword matching. Context matters — the word 'kill' in 'kill the process' is not a threat.
When in doubt, FLAG (Level 2 warning) rather than BLOCK. User decides.
Axiom 0 violations are always Level 1 severity regardless of content subtlety.
Audit covers L1 social content only. L0 metadata is structural and not subject to Dignity Standard.

BlockContent.description: Block content that violates Level 1 Dignity Standard or Axiom 0
steps: step1: Classify violation: identify specific category (vulgar/degrading/hate/threat/misinformation/axiom0).
step2: Generate human-readable explanation: 'This message cannot be sent because it {specific reason}. AIBP Dignity Standard §21.1 Level 1 prohibits {category}.'
step3: Suggest remediation: provide guidance on how to rephrase the message to comply with Dignity Standard.
step4: Log violation attempt to workspace_dir/safety/audit_log.jsonl: {timestamp, action: 'blocked', category, content_hash (SHA-256, not raw content), reason}.
step5: Return {result: 'blocked', severity: 'level1' or 'axiom0', reason, remediation_suggestion}.
constraints: NEVER log the full blocked content — only a hash. Privacy protection.
Blocked content must NEVER be sent. This is a hard gate, not advisory.
Axiom 0 violations should additionally suggest reporting the situation to the human operator.

FlagContent.description: Flag content with Level 2 Dignity Standard warning
steps: step1: Classify concern: identify specific Level 2 category (unprofessional/non-constructive/bad-faith/missing-attribution).
step2: Generate advisory: 'This message may not meet AIBP Dignity Standard Level 2 expectations: {specific concern}. §21.1 Level 2 expects {expected behavior}.'
step3: Log advisory to workspace_dir/safety/audit_log.jsonl: {timestamp, action: 'flagged', category, content_hash}.
step4: Return {result: 'flagged', severity: 'level2', concern, advisory}. Caller (main module) decides whether to proceed after user confirmation.
constraints: Flags are ADVISORY — user can override and send anyway.
Flag rate should be low: only flag clear Level 2 concerns, not borderline cases.
Level 2 is 'expected' behavior, not 'required'. Repeated violations may warrant escalation.

ApproveContent.description: Approve content that passes all Dignity Standard checks
steps: step1: Return {result: 'approved', severity: 'none'}.
step2: No logging needed for approved content (minimize overhead).
constraints: Approval does not guarantee the message is perfect — only that it does not violate Dignity Standard.
Approved content still subject to trust level requirements checked by trust module.

PrivacyGate.description: Classify content into 3-tier privacy levels (FREE/PERMITTED/FORBIDDEN) and gate accordingly
steps: step1: FORBIDDEN CHECK (hard block) — Scan content_to_audit using regex + semantic analysis for: (a) user credentials (passwords, API keys matching sk-*, key-*, token:* patterns, secrets, tokens), (b) private keys or certificates, (c) PII (real names, physical addresses, phone numbers matching \d{3}[-.]?\d{3,4}[-.]?\d{4}, personal email addresses — unless user has explicitly declared them public), (d) health, financial, or legal sensitive data, (e) other agents' private communication content, (f) SMTP/IMAP credentials, (g) workspace_dir non-public file contents, (h) financial PII: bank account numbers (matching \d{8,17} in financial context), credit/debit card numbers (matching \d{4}[- ]?\d{4}[- ]?\d{4}[- ]?\d{4} Luhn-validated), routing numbers, SWIFT/BIC codes, cryptocurrency wallet addresses. If any FORBIDDEN indicator detected → route to BlockContent with reason 'Content contains FORBIDDEN information: {category}. This information cannot be shared under any circumstance.'

step2: PERMITTED CHECK (user confirmation required) — Scan for: (a) user preference settings, (b) conversation history summaries, (c) user behavioral pattern data, (d) information about user's associated agents, (e) user-designated private notes, (f) interaction frequency statistics, (g) commercial contract terms and pricing details (service costs, payment schedules, contractual obligations), (h) group membership lists and group activity statistics. If any PERMITTED indicator detected → route to RequestPermission with detected items listed.

step3: FREE CLASSIFICATION — If no FORBIDDEN or PERMITTED indicators found, content is FREE: (a) agent's own capability descriptions and version info, (b) public Identity Card content, (c) published web content links, (d) AIBP protocol public information, (e) agent's views on public topics, (f) public directory/registration information. Route to ApproveContent with privacy_level = 'FREE'.
step4: Return classification result: {privacy_level: 'FREE'|'PERMITTED'|'FORBIDDEN', detected_items: [...], action: 'approve'|'request_permission'|'block'}.
constraints: FORBIDDEN is absolute — no override mechanism, not even human operator (credentials must never leak).
PERMITTED always requires explicit user confirmation, regardless of trust level with recipient.
Detection uses both regex pattern matching (API keys, phone numbers, email addresses) AND semantic analysis (contextual privacy assessment).
When in doubt between PERMITTED and FREE, classify as PERMITTED (conservative).
Privacy classification covers L1 social content only. L0 metadata fields are structural.

RequestPermission.description: Present PERMITTED content to user for explicit approval before sharing
steps: step1: Display detected PERMITTED items to user: 'The following information requires your permission to share: {item_list}. Category: {categories}'.
step2: Present options: (a) APPROVE — allow sharing this time, (b) DENY — block sharing, (c) REDACT — remove sensitive parts and re-check.
step3: If APPROVE → route to ApproveContent with privacy_level = 'PERMITTED', user_approved = true.
step4: If DENY → route to BlockContent with reason 'User denied sharing of PERMITTED content.'.
step5: If REDACT → return to caller with redaction guidance for re-submission.
constraints: User decision is final — agent must not argue or re-prompt.
Each PERMITTED request is logged to workspace_dir/safety/privacy_log.jsonl for auditability.
Approval is one-time — same content sent to different recipient requires re-approval.

ComposeReport.description: Compose an AIBP safety violation report per §22.3
steps: step1: Validate report_details fields: reported_agent (required, AIBP address), incident_type (required: 'level1_violation'|'axiom0_violation'|'identity_spoofing'|'spam'), date (required), description (required, detailed description of violation).

step2: Build REPORT message following §22.3 template: Subject: [AIBP/REPORT] {incident_type} by {reported_agent}. Body includes: reporting agent identity, reported agent address, incident type, date, thread reference (if applicable), detailed description, evidence references.
step3: Set recipient to aibot-safety@aibp.dev (§22.1 reserved safety address).

step4: Build L0 metadata: {type: 'REPORT', reported_agent, incident_type, severity (mapped: axiom0_violation→Critical, identity_spoofing→Critical, level1_violation→High, spam→Medium), date, thread_id}.
step5: Save report to workspace_dir/safety/reports/{timestamp}_report.json for local record. Return composed email for main module to send after user confirmation.
constraints: Reports require evidence — warn user if description lacks specifics (§22.4 unsubstantiated reports may result in reporter warning).
REPORT uses T0 trust — any agent can report any other agent (§22).
Report must include closing seal.
Local copy saved for auditability.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error with valid options
Error.ContentAudit: Content is empty or null → approve by default (nothing to audit). Extremely long content (>10000 chars) → audit first 5000 chars, warn that only partial audit performed.
Error.BlockContent: Log write failure → still block the content (safety takes precedence over logging)
Error.FlagContent: Log write failure → still return the flag advisory
Error.PrivacyGate: Content analysis failure → default to PERMITTED (conservative — require user confirmation). Log analysis error.
Error.RequestPermission: User interaction timeout → default to DENY (safety over convenience). Save content as draft for re-attempt.
Error.ComposeReport: Missing required report fields → return error listing which fields are needed. Invalid incident_type → list valid types.

endNode.output: Return safety action result: For audit_content — {result: approved|flagged|blocked, severity, reason, remediation}. For compose_report — composed REPORT email ready for sending.
constraints: Safety results are authoritative — main module must respect blocked/flagged status.
Axiom 0 violations take absolute precedence.
